title: Settings

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.themeDesc: Choose a visual style for the interface

setupGuide.title: Welcome — Configure Your LLM
setupGuide.subtitle: Markus needs at least one LLM provider to work. Choose a way to get started:

fromEnv.title: From Environment Variables
fromEnv.description: Automatically detected API keys from .env or system environment.
fromEnv.noKeysHint: No API keys found. Set environment variables like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY and restart the server.

fromOllama.title: Local Ollama
fromOllama.description: Auto-detect a locally running Ollama service and select a model.

fromOpenClaw.title: From OpenClaw Config
fromOpenClaw.description: Import model configs from an existing OpenClaw installation.

manual.title: Manual Configuration
manual.description: Edit ~/.markus/markus.json directly, then restart the server.

browserExtension.title: Install Browser Automation Extension
browserExtension.description: Let AI agents control Chrome to browse, test apps, and inspect pages — no permission dialog each time.
browserExtension.notChrome: Your current browser is not Chrome. Open Markus in Chrome to install the extension.
browserExtension.loadHint: After unzipping, go to Chrome Extensions page → enable "Developer mode" → "Load unpacked" → select the unzipped folder

defaultProvider.title: Default LLM Provider
defaultProvider.primaryProviderDesc: Used for all agent interactions unless overridden
defaultProvider.autoFallbackDesc: When enabled, automatically try other providers/models if the primary one fails. When disabled, only use the selected model — errors will not trigger fallback.

modelRouting.title: Model Routing
modelRouting.defaultModelDesc: Used as fallback when no specific model is assigned for a task.
modelRouting.taskAssignDesc: Each task type uses the assigned model. Unassigned tasks use the default model above.
modelRouting.addProviderHint: add a provider that supports this capability above
modelRouting.staleProvider: Provider not configured — clear or reconfigure
modelRouting.noProvidersHint: Configure at least one provider above to assign models to tasks.

modelPicker.empty: No models available for this provider.

modelProviders.title: Model Providers & Pricing
modelProviders.providerNameHint: Use anthropic, openai, google, ollama for first-party; any other name uses OpenAI-compatible API
modelProviders.notConfiguredHint: Not configured. Use the "Add Provider" button below, or set API keys in environment variables.
modelProviders.quickSetupHint: Enter your API Key to start using this provider immediately.
modelProviders.catalogModelsAvailable: {{count}} more models supported by this provider

networkProxy.title: Network Proxy
networkProxy.description: Configure an HTTP proxy for ChatGPT OAuth login and Codex API calls. Other LLM providers use system/TUN proxy automatically.

oauthAuth.title: OAuth Authentication
oauthAuth.description: Sign in with your ChatGPT account to use Codex models via your subscription (no API key required).
oauthAuth.deviceInstructions: Open the verification URL and enter the code:
oauthAuth.manualCallbackHint: If the browser didn't open or you're on a remote machine, paste the callback URL below:

autoDetect.title: Auto-detect & Import
autoDetect.description: Discover LLM providers from environment variables, local Ollama service, or existing configurations.

ollama.title: Local Ollama
ollama.description: Auto-detect a locally running Ollama service and configure it as a model provider. No API key required.
ollama.notRunning: Ollama is not running locally. Start Ollama first, then click detect.

envConfig.title: Environment Variable Configuration
envConfig.description: Detect model API keys from environment variables and write them to the config file.
envConfig.noKeysFound: No model API keys found in environment variables
envConfig.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one provider to apply

openClaw.title: Import from OpenClaw
openClaw.description: Detect and import LLM configurations from an existing OpenClaw installation.

agentExecution.title: Agent Execution
agentExecution.maxToolIterationsDesc: Safety cap on tool call loops per agent turn (0 = unlimited, applies to all agents and subagents)

cognitive.title: Cognitive Preparation Pipeline
cognitive.description: Before each main LLM call, the agent runs a lightweight appraisal to assess the stimulus and prepare persona-aware context. This can improve response relevance at the cost of a small extra LLM call (~500ms).
cognitive.enabledDesc: When enabled, agents run a cognitive appraisal before responding
cognitive.maxDepthDesc: Controls how much preparation is done before each response

searchApi.title: Web Search API Keys
searchApi.description: Configure search API keys so agents can search the web for real-time information. Priority: Serper > Tavily > Bing > Google > SerpAPI > Brave > Exa > Bocha > DuckDuckGo (free fallback).
searchApi.bingHint: Microsoft Bing search via Azure Cognitive Services
searchApi.googleHint: Google Programmable Search Engine (requires API key + Search Engine ID)
searchApi.bochaHint: AI-optimized search for Chinese & global content via bochaai.com
searchApi.failedToSave: Failed to save search API keys
searchApi.freeBackend: DuckDuckGo is always available as a free fallback (no API key needed).

browserAutomation.title: Browser Automation
browserAutomation.description: Settings for the Chrome DevTools browser skill. Agents use Chrome to browse, test web apps, and inspect pages.
browserAutomation.bringToFrontDesc: When enabled, Chrome tabs will be brought to the foreground when agents navigate. When disabled (default), agents work silently in the background without stealing focus.
browserAutomation.autoCloseTabsDesc: Automatically close agent-owned browser tabs when the agent task completes or the agent is removed.
browserAutomation.remoteDebuggingPortDesc: Set a persistent debugging port (e.g. 9222) to avoid Chrome's permission dialog every time an agent connects.
browserAutomation.remoteDebuggingPortDescLead: Set a persistent debugging port (e.g. 9222) to avoid Chrome's permission dialog every time an agent connects. Start Chrome with
browserAutomation.remoteDebuggingPortDescTail: then set the same port here. Set to 0 to use auto-connect (requires manual permission approval).
browserAutomation.tabsForeground: Tabs will be brought to foreground
browserAutomation.usingPort: Using persistent connection on port {{port}} (no more permission dialogs)
browserAutomation.usingAutoConnect: Using auto-connect mode (permission dialog required on each connection)
browserAutomation.autoClickAllowDialogDesc: Chrome shows an "Allow remote debugging?" dialog each time Markus connects. When enabled, Markus auto-clicks "Allow" via OS accessibility APIs.
browserAutomation.autoClickAllowDialogMacNote: macOS: Grant Accessibility permission in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility for the app running Markus (Markus.app / Terminal / iTerm).
browserAutomation.autoClickAllowDialogWinNote: Windows: No additional permissions needed. Uses built-in UI Automation.
browserAutomation.autoClickAllowDialogLinuxNote: Linux: Not yet supported. Use Remote Debugging Port (above) as an alternative.
browserAutomation.autoClickTestSuccess: Auto-click verified! Successfully connected to Chrome and loaded a test page ({{title}}).
browserAutomation.autoClickTestNoPermission: Accessibility permission not granted. The system settings page has been opened — please add this app to the list.
browserAutomation.autoClickTestChromeNotRunning: Chrome is not running. Please start Chrome first.
browserAutomation.autoClickTestUnsupported: Auto-click is not supported on this platform. Use Remote Debugging Port instead.
browserAutomation.modeExtensionDesc: Browser tools route through the extension — no debugging dialog, no extra permissions, works when screen is locked.
browserAutomation.modeDebuggingPortDesc: Connected via persistent debugging port — no permission dialog on each connection.
browserAutomation.modeAutoClickDesc: Using chrome-devtools-mcp with OS auto-click to handle the debugging dialog. May not work when screen is locked.
browserAutomation.modeManualDesc: Chrome will show a permission dialog each time an agent connects. You must click Allow manually.
browserAutomation.extensionStatusDesc: Enables browser automation without the debugging dialog. No Accessibility permissions needed, works even when screen is locked.
browserAutomation.extensionActiveNote: Extension active — browser tools route through extension (no debugging dialog)
browserAutomation.extensionStep1Desc: Download the zip and extract it to any location (e.g. Desktop)
browserAutomation.extensionStep2Desc: Open the extensions management page in Chrome
browserAutomation.extensionStep3Desc: Enable "Developer mode" (top right toggle), click "Load unpacked", and select the folder extracted in step 1
browserAutomation.extensionOpenError: Failed to open Chrome extensions page

dataStorage.title: Data & Storage
dataStorage.orphanedDesc: These directories have no matching database record and were likely left behind when agents or teams were deleted.

browserOpened: Browser opened for authorization. Complete the login in the browser window...

remoteAccess.title: Remote Access
remoteAccess.description: Access this Markus instance from your phone or any device via WebRTC P2P connection through Markus Hub.
remoteAccess.loginRequired: Sign in to Markus Hub to enable remote access.
remoteAccess.scanQr: Scan with your phone camera to connect

userManagement.title: Local Markus
userManagement.sharedInstanceDesc: Manage user accounts on this machine's Markus instance. All accounts share the same workspace, AI agents, and teams. For example: on a shared Linux server, multiple colleagues each log in with their own account to the same Markus.
userManagement.orgHint: If team members each use their own Markus on separate computers, go to the "Organization & License" tab to invite members and share an enterprise license.
userManagement.name: Name
userManagement.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to remove this user? This action cannot be undone.
userManagement.cannotDeleteSelf: You cannot delete your own account
userManagement.inviteLinkGenerated: Invite link generated — copy and send to the user
userManagement.copyInviteLink: Send this link to the user so they can set up their account:

license.title: License
license.expiredWarning: License has expired. Enterprise features are no longer available.
license.renewalHint: Contact sales for a new license key and enter it below to renew.
license.activateDesc: Have a license key? Enter it to unlock all Enterprise features.
license.trialDesc: Full access to all Enterprise features. No credit card required. Reverts to Free automatically.
license.importDesc: For air-gapped deployments, import a .lic license file.
license.contactSalesDesc: Copy the following info and send it to sales ({{email}}) to receive your license key.
license.deactivateDesc: Reverts to Free plan. All Enterprise features will become unavailable.
license.deactivateConfirm: Your plan will revert to Free and all Enterprise features will become unavailable. Continue?
license.freeFeaturesDesc: Basic agent capabilities with usage limits
license.trialAlreadyUsed: Trial has already been used for this account.
license.licenseNotFound: License not found or not active.
license.instanceLimitReached: Maximum number of instances reached for this license.
license.notOwnedHint: This license is managed by the organization owner. License details are only visible to the license owner.

account.title: Organization & License
account.subtitle: Manage your organization membership, license, and local Markus users.

org.title: My Organization
org.description: Manage your organization on Markus Hub. Organization members each install Markus on their own computer and share a single enterprise license. For example: a team of 5 people, each running an independent Markus instance on their own laptop, sharing the organization's license quota.
org.usersHint: If your team runs Markus on one shared server (e.g. a single Linux server used by multiple people), go to the "Local Markus" tab to add local user accounts instead.
org.seatsFullHint: All seats are occupied. Contact sales for more seats.
org.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address
org.errorUserNotFound: No user found for this email, invitation email has been sent
org.errorAlreadyMember: This user is already a member or has a pending invitation
org.errorAlreadySent: An invitation has already been sent to this email
org.leaveConfirmMsg: Are you sure you want to leave "{{name}}"? You will lose access to this organization's resources and will need to be re-invited to rejoin.
org.noOrg: No organization found. Connect to Markus Hub to get started.
org.popupBlocked: Popup blocked by browser. Please allow popups for this site and try again.
org.loadFailed: Failed to load organizations. Please check your network.
org.noOrgAfterConnect: Connected successfully, but no organizations found. You may need to create one on Markus Hub.

feishu.description: Configure Feishu integration for notifications and approvals
feishu.longConnectionMode: Using long connection mode (WebSocket) — no public IP required
feishu.enableHint: When enabled, Markus will establish a long connection to Feishu and start processing events
feishu.notifyChatIdHint: Select a group chat, or use private messages (default).
feishu.forwardApprovalsHint: Send approval requests to Feishu as interactive cards
feishu.forwardNotificationsHint: Send system notifications to Feishu chat
feishu.priorityHint: Only notifications with the selected priority levels will be forwarded
feishu.setupGuideDesc: Follow these steps to set up the Feishu integration:
feishu.guideStep1: Go to Feishu Open Platform (open.feishu.cn) and create an enterprise self-built app
feishu.guideStep2: Go to App → Features → Bot, enable bot capability
feishu.guideStep3: Go to App → Permissions, enable im:message and im:message.receive_v1
feishu.guideStep4: Go to App → Events & Callbacks → Subscription mode, select "Use long connection"
feishu.guideStep5: On the Events & Callbacks page, subscribe to "Receive messages v2.0" (im.message.receive_v1)
feishu.guideStep6: Go to App → Credentials, copy App ID and App Secret into the fields above
feishu.guideStep7: Click "Test Connection" to verify credentials, then enable the integration
feishu.guideStep8: Add the bot to a group chat (or send a direct message to the bot)
feishu.longConnectionNote: Long connection mode requires an enterprise self-built app. Store apps are not supported. No public IP or domain is needed.
feishu.oneClickDesc: Scan a QR code with Feishu to automatically create and configure the app. No manual setup needed.
feishu.scanToCreate: Scan QR Code to Create App
feishu.oneClickPermissions: The app will be created with permissions: bot messaging, message receiving, user info reading, and group chat management.
feishu.scanHint: Use your Feishu mobile app to scan and authorize. The app will be created in your organization.
feishu.registerSuccess: App created successfully! Integration is now active.
feishu.chatIdHint: Chat ID can be found in the Feishu group chat URL or via the API
feishu.noChatHint: No groups found. Add the bot to a group chat to enable group notifications.
feishu.noChatFallbackHint: No group selected — notifications will be sent to you as private messages.
feishu.fillRequired: Please fill in App ID and App Secret first
